steps.title: Identify the narrative shift from struggle to understanding
steps.content:
The passage first describes Dillard's struggle to reconcile contradictory aspects of nature—the beautiful delicacy of a single fish versus the brutal cruelty of a swarm.

The sentence after the blank reveals her ultimate understanding of how these opposites relate.

The key signal is this progression from problem to resolution.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
”Ultimately” marks the final understanding reached after grappling with contradiction—it shows that Dillard's ultimate realization is that nature's intricacy and harshness are inextricably linked.

The other three either suggest methodology (“To that end”), add unrelated information (“Moreover”), or imply direct logical consequence rather than the narrative arc from struggle to final insight (“Hence”).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
